content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as layered "curiosity filters" that prioritize which new environmental experiences get encoded into long-term memory, rather than just passive storage structures for accumulated knowledge. This design aligns with the core drive of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning, where memory itself becomes an active selection mechanism that amplifies intrinsic exploration instead of just recording it.
